methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe for valuation is constrained: seven private companies operate in this sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public companies serve as direct benchmarks. The sector remains nascent with limited public market data.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Rhoman Aerospace. The private peer set of seven companies lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables to support a reliable median calculation. Filtering for seed-stage peers within the Drones & Autonomous sector yielded zero datapoints; similarly, adjacent-stage and similar-valuation-tier filters produced no qualifying matches. Attempting to apply an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in company maturity, technology focus, and capital efficiency across the peer universe.

This analysis is constrained by data insufficiency. Without applicable triangulation methods, no supported valuation range can be derived from the provided dataset. A credible valuation would require either (1) expansion of the comparable set through adjacent sectors or earlier-stage company data, (2) application of fundamentals-based methods (discounted cash flow or comparable transaction analysis), or (3) scenario modeling of technical and commercial milestones. The founders Thomas Youmans and Thomas Callen should be evaluated primarily on prior track record and product-market fit signals rather than peer benchmarking.
